req.title: Delete patient (soft) (audit)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-079 mandates three verification axes: (1) happy-path soft-delete by an admin preserves history and emits an AuditEvent, (2) rejection with 403 when a non-admin caller attempts deletion, and (3) boundary behaviour when the patient resource is accessed via _history immediately after deletion to confirm exactly one new history version is appended. STC covers the active→deleted state transition. EP covers the valid-admin partition. NEG covers the unauthorized-caller partition. BVA covers the version-count boundary (versionId increments by exactly 1). An additional DT test covers the compound decision: admin role × delete operation × AuditEvent emission all true simultaneously.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_marks_resource_deleted_and_emits_audit
preconditions: A Patient resource exists with a known id
actions: GET /Patient/{id}/_history to confirm resource is still retrievable
GET /AuditEvent?entity={id}&action=D to confirm audit record exists
expected: GET /Patient/{id}/_history returns bundle with at least one entry
AuditEvent bundle contains at least one entry with action=D for this patient
trace: R-HLT-079: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_soft_delete_marks_resource_deleted_and_emits_audit(
    admin_client,
    create_patient,
):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    delete_resp = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    get_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 410

    history_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert history_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = history_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1 or len(bundle.get("entry", [])) >= 1

    audit_resp = admin_client.get(
        "/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}", "action": "D"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_bundle = audit_resp.json()
    entries = audit_bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "Expected at least one AuditEvent for delete action"

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_returns_403
preconditions: A Patient resource exists with a known id
expected: Patient resource is NOT marked deleted (still returns 200 on GET)
No AuditEvent with action=D is created for this attempt (or an access-denied event is recorded)
trace: R-HLT-079: error pathway 403 when not admin
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_delete_returns_403(
    admin_client,
    practitioner_client,
    create_patient,
):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    delete_resp = practitioner_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code == 403

    get_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Patient must still exist after unauthorized delete"

    audit_resp = admin_client.get(
        "/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}", "action": "D"},
    )
    if audit_resp.status_code == 200:
        entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
        successful_deletes = [
            e for e in entries
            if e.get("resource", {}).get("outcome") == "0"
        ]
        assert len(successful_deletes) == 0, "No successful delete AuditEvent expected"

tests.name: test_history_version_increments_by_exactly_one_after_soft_delete
preconditions: A Patient resource exists with a known id and a recorded current versionId
expected: The maximum versionId in _history equals pre-delete versionId + 1 (boundary: exactly one increment)
The latest history entry has meta.tag or status indicating deleted
Total history entry count equals pre-delete count + 1
trace: R-HLT-079: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_history_version_increments_by_exactly_one_after_soft_delete(
    admin_client,
    create_patient,
):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    get_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    pre_version = int(get_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"])

    history_before = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}/_history").json()
    count_before = len(history_before.get("entry", []))

    delete_resp = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    history_after = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}/_history").json()
    entries_after = history_after.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries_after) == count_before + 1, "History must grow by exactly 1"

    versions = [int(e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"]) for e in entries_after]
    assert max(versions) == pre_version + 1, "versionId must increment by exactly 1"

tests.name: test_admin_delete_decision_table_all_conditions_true
preconditions: Patient resource exists and is in active state
actions: Assert GET /Patient/{id}/_history returns bundle with deleted entry
Assert AuditEvent exists with action=D and outcome=success
expected: All three conditions simultaneously satisfied: role=admin AND resource deleted AND AuditEvent emitted
AuditEvent agent references the admin actor
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_delete_decision_table_all_conditions_true(
    admin_client,
    create_patient,
    admin_user_id,
):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_client)

    del_resp = admin_client.delete(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204), "Condition: delete accepted"

    gone_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert gone_resp.status_code == 410, "Condition: resource marked deleted"

    hist_resp = admin_client.get(f"/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1, "Condition: history preserved"

    audit_resp = admin_client.get(
        "/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}", "action": "D"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "Condition: AuditEvent emitted"
    latest = entries[-1]["resource"]
    assert latest.get("outcome") == "0", "Condition: AuditEvent outcome = success"
    agents = latest.get("agent", [])
    agent_refs = [a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "") for a in agents]
    assert any(admin_user_id in ref for ref in agent_refs), "Condition: admin actor recorded"
